body: PT Sarana Multi Infrastruktur (no ticker) will issue a shelf-registration bond worth IDR 2.7 trillion in early December 2024, with proceeds to be used for infrastructure financing, split into four series with varied yields ranging from 6.30% to 7% per annum. The underwriters assigned to the bond issuance include PT BNI Sekuritas, PT BRI Danareksa Sekuritas, PT DBS Vickers Sekuritas Indonesia, PT Indo Premier Sekuritas, and PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM).
title: PT Sarana Multi Infrastruktur to Issue IDR 2.7 Trillion Shelf-Registration Bond in Early December 2024 for Infrastructure Financing.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (IHSG) demonstrated an optimistic trend, opening at 7,147.26 points and climbing to 7,206.84 points, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESG also showing gains. Large-cap stocks including Barito Pacific Tbk (BAPA), Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMIN), and Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM) contributed to the morning's gains, while others like Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) and Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) declined.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Demonstrates Optimistic Trend

body: PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia (AADI) is set to initiate its public offering on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, offering 778.68 million shares, or 10% of its total issued and paid-up capital, at a price range of IDR 4,590 to IDR 5,900 per share, potentially raising IDR 4.59 trillion in fresh funds. The bookbuilding process is scheduled to take place from November 12 to 18, followed by the public offering from November 29 to December 3, and listing on the IDX on December 5, 2024, with PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk as the lead underwriter.
title: Adaro Andalan Indonesia to Raise IDR 4.59 Trillion in IPO on Indonesia Stock Exchange.

body: PT Logindo Samudera Makmur Tbk (LOGD) will pay off a USD 20 million loan by converting it into 1.75 billion shares at IDR 25 each, increasing its paid-up capital to 144.99 billion shares from 101.24 billion shares. The company's financial metrics as of June 2024 show total assets of USD 126.15 million, total liabilities of USD 102.60 million, and revenue of USD 22.01 million, with a gross profit of USD 7.18 million and an operating profit of USD 4.07 million.
title: PT Logindo Samudera Makmur to Pay USD 20 Million Loan via Debt Conversion and Share Issuance

body: PT Metrodata Electronic Tbk (MTDL) expects its smartphone distribution business line to grow 13% this year, driven by a shift in middle-class consumer behavior towards cheaper products, while its corporate business line has also improved in Q3 2023. Despite a decline in purchase power, the company remains optimistic about its performance in Q4 2023 and 2025, citing a downtrend in interest rates that will benefit its customers in the real sector.
title: Metrodata Electronic's Smartphone Distribution Business Sees 13% Growth Amid Weakened Purchase Power

body: PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk (TAPG) received an interim dividend of IDR 586.17 billion from its subsidiary PT Agro Multi Persada, in which it owns a 94.93% stake. As of September 30, 2024, TAPG reported revenue of IDR 6.24 trillion and a net profit of IDR 1.68 trillion, with the dividend distribution not expected to have a material impact on the company.
title: Triputra Agro Persada receives IDR 586.17 billion in interim dividend from subsidiary PT Agro Multi Persada.

body: Winfly Ltd. increased its stake in PT Bank MNC Internasional Tbk (BAB) to 10.59% by purchasing 256.35 million shares, while PT MNC Sekuritas acquired 34.56 million shares of PT MNC Land Tbk, holding a 14.82% ownership stake. Other notable transactions include PT Sinarmas Cakrawala raising its stake in PT Sinarmas Multiartha Tbk, PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk acquiring shares of PT Elnusa Tbk, and PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk buying its own shares.
title: Multiple investors adjust their stakes in various Indonesian companies across different sectors.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 843.06 million shares, with notable sales including 745.97 million shares of PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) and 326.89 million shares of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO). Meanwhile, top buys included 608.88 million shares of PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) and 75.67 million shares of PT MNC Land Tbk (MNCL), among others.
title: Foreign investors sell 4.67 billion shares in Indonesian market, led by sales of Bumi Resources and GoTo shares.

body: PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) has signed a syndicated loan agreement with several banks, including PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, PT Sarana Multi Infrastruktur, and PT Bank Digital BCA, for a maximum of IDR 10 trillion, which will be used to refinance existing credit facilities and for working capital. The loan facility has a tenor of 7 years with an interest rate of 3 months JIBOR + specified margin, and will also be utilized for auctioning frequency band spectrum.
title: Smartfren Telecom signs IDR 10 trillion syndicated loan agreement with several banks to refinance existing credit facilities and fund working capital.

body: The Ministry of State-Owned Enterprises has appointed Captain Wamildan Tsani Panjaitan as the President Director of PT Garuda Indonesia Tbk (GIAA), replacing Irfan Setiaputra, leveraging Panjaitan's 20 years of experience in the aviation industry. Prior to this appointment, Panjaitan worked at PT Batik Air Indonesia and PT Lion Air, where he held positions such as Director of Safety, Security, and Quality, and CEO, respectively.
title: Ministry of State-Owned Enterprises appoints Captain Wamildan Tsani Panjaitan as new President Director of Garuda Indonesia.

body: PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) divested its shares in PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk, worth IDR 22.48 billion (4.71% of total issued and paid-up capital), and PT Bali Media Telekomunikasi, worth IDR 562.16 billion, to redistribute its investment portfolio. The company also signed a USD 525 million loan facility with a 3-year tenor, which can be repaid through a debt-to-equity swap or compensation from relevant interest, aiming to focus on systematic business development and investment portfolio management.
title: PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa divests shares in PT Smartfren Telecom to redistribute investment portfolio.

body: The Indonesian Ministry of Manpower is encouraging collaborative workforce development programs with publicly listed companies to boost labor competency, with a focus on competency improvement, workforce placement expansion, and labor welfare protection enhancement over the next five years. The Ministry is expecting CEOs of top companies, including those from banking (PT Bank Central Asia Tbk), mining, and telecommunications, to drive labor skill improvement through collaborations with various parties, including employment programs.
title: Ministry of Manpower encourages public companies to collaborate on workforce development programs to boost labor competency.

body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) saw a significant sell-off of 1.65 billion shares, contributing to a net foreign volume drop of -1.16 billion shares, while PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) experienced a net volume increase of 65.09 million shares. Other notable transactions included PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with a net volume drop of -394.17 million shares, and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ASTR) with a net volume increase of 24.82 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell majority of shares in Indonesian companies, including GoTo and Bumi Resources, on Thursday.

body: PT Semen Indonesia Tbk (SMGR) has successfully implemented an initiative to convert urban solid waste into refuse-derived fuel, replacing coal fuel, and has been recognized with the Best CEO Award for its sustainability commitments. The company reported a net profit of IDR 2.17 trillion in 2024 and has consistently distributed cash dividends, resulting in a dividend yield of 2.25%, solidifying its position in the Tempo-IDNFinancials 52 Index.
title: PT Semen Indonesia's CEO Wins Award for Innovative Urban Waste-to-Fuel Initiative

body: Andrianto Oetomo, President Director of PT Dharma Satya Nusantara Tbk (DSNG), was named Best CEO at the Top CEO Indonesia Awards 2024 for his leadership in optimizing the use of bio-CNG alternative energy, resulting in a reduction of 184.09 tons of CO2 emissions and savings of Rp30.94 billion. In 2023, DSNG recorded sales of Rp9.49 trillion, net profit of Rp839.80 billion, and EBITDA of Rp2.42 trillion, showing growth despite challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic.
title: Andrianto Oetomo, President Director of PT Dharma Satya Nugraha, Wins Best CEO Award for Bio-CNG Alternative Energy Initiative.
